one_time_description: Specific Claims settlement receipt recognized as revenue in 2023
economic_notes: Group operates logging, gravel pit (Painted Rock Aggregates), retail store and fuel outlet (Big Sky Station), utility services (Ckukutusem), natural resources LLP, and holdings LLP. Pension plan contributions of $60,623 in 2023.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) management has not completed PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations assessment so tangible capital assets and accumulated surplus may be understated; (2) certain GBEs/GBPs have not prepared financial statements under IFRS as required by PSAS. The Group has 5 partnerships accounted for by modified equity method and 3 GBEs (KDC, Big Sky Station, Skeetchestn Natural Resources Corporation). Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($4,691,184) + ASETS ($99,599) + BC First Nations Gaming ($524,759) + CMHC ($3,757,256) + FCRSA ($995,189) + FNESC ($778,721) + FNHA ($69,148) + New Relationship Trust ($2,400) + Province of BC ($490,929) + Qwemtsin Health Society ($282,587) + Secwepemc Child & Family Services ($582,668) + Shuswap Nation Tribal Council ($329,163) + Specific Claims ($538623) = $13,141,026 approximately; OSR adjusted accordingly. Education expenses combined School ($1,377,049) and Education ($950,120). Housing combined Housing ($475,837) and Social Housing ($337,460). Cash includes $17,542,268 in short-term GICs. Funds held in trust of $6,258,300 relate to Smith/Curtis Specific Claim Settlement. No schedule of remuneration found in the extracted text.
